title: Agent OS ROI review: generated-surface trust for ordinary agents
goal: Capture the ROI case for exposing generated-surface trust to ordinary agents.
Separate daily trust affordance from maintainer-only generation machinery.
scope: Issue #1115 ROI point 4: generated-surface trust for ordinary agents.
Generated files, source commands, stale status, and direct-edit prohibitions.
non_goals: Do not change generated outputs in this review.
Do not productize generated-surface trust as a standalone package.

review_mode.review question: Should generated-surface provenance and staleness be ordinary agent context rather than maintainer-only detail?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: Issue #1115 body, docs/agent-os-capabilities.md generated-surface trust section, earlier stale schema reference proof failure

review_method.commands used: make maintainer-surfaces; uv run agentic-workspace implement --changed <ten review artifact paths> --task "Create one planning review artifact for each of the ten agent OS capability ROI points from issue #1115" --format json
review_method.evidence sources: GitHub #1115; docs/agent-os-capabilities.md; make maintainer-surfaces stale docs/reference/proof-selection-rules.md result

findings.title: Generated-surface trust is daily agent context, not only maintainer machinery
findings.summary: The capability document currently frames generated-surface trust mostly as internal maintainer/development machinery. Ordinary agents still need a compact answer to whether a touched file is generated, where it comes from, how to refresh it, and whether direct edits are wrong.
findings.evidence: Issue #1115 ranks this fourth by ROI. The earlier `make maintainer-surfaces` run failed because `docs/reference/proof-selection-rules.md` was stale and named the refresh command. That is exactly the kind of signal an ordinary agent should receive before deciding how to edit or validate a generated file.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents may manually edit generated artifacts, miss refresh commands, or mistrust useful generated surfaces because provenance and freshness are not exposed at the point of use.
findings.suggested action: Create a child slice that exposes generated-source, freshness, refresh command, and direct-edit policy in ordinary workspace/doctor/implement output for changed paths.
findings.promotion target: GitHub child issue under #1115; Generated-Surface Trust with Workspace/report exposure
findings.promotion trigger: Promote when stale generated surfaces or direct-edit ambiguity recur, or when generated command extraction needs a daily-use trust affordance.
findings.post-remediation note shape: shrink to pointer to generated-surface trust child issue and maintainer proof output

recommendation.promote: High-ROI child slice; especially valuable when changed-path routing touches generated references.
recommendation.defer: May pair naturally with change-impact mapping.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not dismiss while stale generated references remain a live proof class.

retention.trigger: Ordinary agent output exposes generated-surface trust status or a child issue owns it.

validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
drift_log: 2026-05-24: Review record created from issue #1115 ROI point 4.
